headline: Weakened at home, Macron counts on 'his' last G7 to boost his diplomatic record
description: As his second term grows increasingly complex at home amid the killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna, the French president, who is chairing the G7 summit in Evian, is staking his legitimacy on an ambitious international agenda.

headline: After the US-Iran deal, Israel revels in its military might but finds itself at a strategic impasse

Air quality and global warming: Moving beyond an apparent paradox
description: For several decades, many countries have reduced air pollution, improving public health. However, the decrease in sulfur aerosols – particles that reflect the sun's rays – has intensified global warming.

headline: In Kyiv, a strike damages the Dormition Cathedral at the heart of the historic Kyiv Pechersk Lavra
description: Ukrainian officials accuse Russia of deliberately targeting cultural landmarks symbolizing Ukrainian identity, as heavy strikes hit the country from Sunday night into Monday.

headline: Dominique Méda: 'We must make the green transition desirable'
description: In 1972, the French Communist Party sharply criticized a call for responsible environmentalism. Today, it is the far right that brands all green policies as punitive, notes Dominique Méda. She urges showcasing the benefits of the green transition for everyone.

headline: Mbappé, the French team's adored and criticized superstar
description: The captain of Les Bleus, who begin their quest for a third World Cup title on Tuesday, boasts impressive statistics for both club and country. However, in recent years, his public image has declined.

The Swiss canton attracting Dubai's ultra-wealthy
description: Known for its favorable tax system, Zug is attracting rich individuals looking for security and stability. The trend is also spreading to other regions of the country.

headline: The Knicks, the soul of New York, are NBA champions after more than half a century of waiting
description: After a long period of anticipation and frustration, New York, where basketball is so much more than just a sport, finally celebrates, following its 94-90 triumph on Saturday in Game 5 of the NBA Finals against Victor Wembanyama's Spurs.

headline: Marjane Satrapi's comic strips for Le Monde, 20 years ago
description: Best known for her masterpiece 'Persepolis,' the French-Iranian graphic novelist and filmmaker, who died on June 4, published comic strips in issues of Le Monde's magazine.
